framework: A path qualifies only if you can point at companies hiring for it or a role title that demonstrably exists - not a blog asserting it matters. Ranked by strength of that evidence, not by how often the skill is praised. Curated by hand; this file is editorial, which is why it is NOT inside <role>.json (that bundle is DOL-derived only).

paths.path: Ship a working thing, not only a recommendation
paths.what_it_is: Build and deploy something the client actually runs, rather than delivering the deck that describes it.
paths.why_it_signals: BCG X, a 3,000+ person unit, exists on this distinction: it builds and deploys production-ready software rather than stopping at analytical models.

paths.what_it_is: Run the analysis and build the model, rather than briefing someone else to do it.
paths.why_it_signals: McKinsey QuantumBlack (~1,700 people, 40+ offices) and Bain's Advanced Analytics Group are dedicated, separately-recruited units for precisely this.

paths.path: Own something in production after the engagement ends
paths.what_it_is: Be able to run and maintain what you delivered, not just hand it over.
paths.why_it_signals: Across engineering hiring, having only built rather than operated a system is the most-cited disqualifier; it transfers directly to technology consulting.
